description: Single-screen operator landing. Status of every component plus current pipeline + alert stats. Drill-down links at the bottom.

links.title: Prefect UI
links.tooltip: Open Prefect 3 flow-runs UI (tailscale workstation IP, port 4200)

links.title: Langfuse UI
links.tooltip: Open Langfuse 3 — LLM traces, prompts, experiments (tailscale workstation IP, port 3010)

links.title: Pyroscope UI
links.tooltip: Continuous profiling — CPU + memory flamegraphs (tailscale workstation IP, port 4040)

links.title: GlitchTip UI
links.tooltip: Error tracking + Sentry-compatible event viewer (tailscale workstation IP, port 8080)

links.title: Alertmanager
links.tooltip: Prometheus alert routing + silencing (tailscale workstation IP, port 9093)

links.title: Uptime Kuma
links.tooltip: External-service uptime monitor (tailscale workstation IP, port 3002)

links.title: Prometheus
links.tooltip: Raw PromQL UI (Grafana usually wraps this) (tailscale workstation IP, port 9091)

links.title: Tempo
links.tooltip: Tempo datasource — opens Grafana Explore view (Loki/Tempo have no standalone UI)

links.title: Loki
links.tooltip: Loki datasource — opens Grafana Explore view (Loki/Tempo have no standalone UI)

links.title: pgAdmin
links.tooltip: Postgres admin UI (databases, queries, schema) (tailscale workstation IP, port 18443)

links.title: Worker API
links.tooltip: FastAPI backend health + status endpoints (tailscale workstation IP, port 8002)

links.title: ✏ Talk to Poindexter (LiveKit)
links.tooltip: Self-hosted LiveKit web client (poindexter#389). Same-origin wss via Tailscale Funnel — no mixed-content. 7-day token minted server-side; tap Connect, allow mic. Multi-party-capable: voice-agent-livekit + you in the same ‘poindexter’ room.

panels.description: Worker FastAPI service responding to /metrics. If 0, the content pipeline is offline.
panels.title: Worker

panels.description: Postgres reachable via the local-brain-db datasource. If 0 the dashboard itself would fail — this is a belt+suspenders sanity check.

targets.rawSql: SELECT 1 AS "Postgres";

panels.title: Postgres

panels.description: nvidia-smi exporter. Missing = GPU not detected, degraded, or exporter down.
panels.title: GPU

panels.description: Tempo traces backend.
panels.title: Tempo

panels.description: Pyroscope profiling server. Non-critical (profiling is best-effort).
panels.title: Pyroscope

panels.description: Uptime-kuma external health monitor.
panels.title: Uptime

panels.description: content_tasks created in the last 24h, bucketed by hour. Flat for >2h means autonomous topic discovery has stalled.

targets.rawSql: SELECT $__timeGroup(created_at, $__interval) AS time, COUNT(*) AS "Tasks" FROM content_tasks WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Tasks (24h)

panels.description: Approval queue fill % vs max_approval_queue setting. When this hits 100, new generation throttles until you review.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM content_tasks WHERE status='awaiting_approval')::numeric / NULLIF((SELECT value::int FROM app_settings WHERE key='max_approval_queue'), 0), 0) AS "Queue % of cap";

panels.title: Approval %

panels.description: Hours since the most recent publish. Green if <48h, yellow 48h-7d, red >7d. Bargauge fills toward 168h (1 week).

targets.rawSql: SELECT EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (NOW() - MAX(published_at)))/3600 AS "Hours since publish" FROM posts WHERE status='published';

panels.title: Last publish (h)

panels.description: Distinct alerts firing in the dashboard time range. Deduped by fingerprint — a single alert that re-fires every 15 min counts once, not four times. Change the dashboard time range to widen/narrow the window.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT fingerprint) AS "Firing" FROM alert_events WHERE status='firing' AND $__timeFilter(received_at);

panels.title: Alerts (1h)

panels.description: Count of is_secret=true app_settings rows that are stored as PLAINTEXT (not encrypted at rest). Should be 0 — any row with is_secret=true should be encrypted with pgcrypto via plugins/secrets. Non-zero means a secret is stored in the clear and should be re-encrypted.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Plaintext secrets" FROM app_settings WHERE is_secret = true AND value != '' AND value NOT LIKE 'enc:v1:%';

panels.title: Plain secrets

panels.description: Tasks rejected in the last 24h by a hard veto (citation_verifier / programmatic_validator / topic_delivery). Thresholds are calibrated to the autonomous topic-discovery baseline — expect 5-20/day during normal operation. Red only if the number suggests a regression (a new false-positive class is in play).

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Hard rejects" FROM content_tasks WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) AND status = 'rejected' AND (error_message ILIKE '%veto: programmatic_validator%' OR error_message ILIKE '%veto: citation_verifier%' OR error_message ILIKE '%veto: topic_delivery%');

panels.title: Hard rejects
Recently published
panels.description: Last 5 posts on the public site, newest first.

targets.rawSql: SELECT title AS "Title",        COALESCE(c.name, 'uncategorized') AS "Category",        p.published_at AS "Published",        p.view_count AS "Views" FROM posts p LEFT JOIN categories c ON c.id = p.category_id WHERE p.status='published' ORDER BY p.published_at DESC LIMIT 5

panels.title: Top category
panels.description: Most-published category (name + count). Was a pie chart, but the data is monotype right now — one category dominates, so the slice was always 100%. Stat is honest about that.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COALESCE(c.name, 'uncategorized') AS metric, COUNT(*)::int AS value FROM posts p LEFT JOIN categories c ON c.id = p.category_id WHERE p.status='published' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC LIMIT 1

panels.title: LLM spend
panels.description: Daily LLM cost in USD from cost_logs. Range respects the dashboard time picker — change it at the top right to widen or narrow the window.

targets.rawSql: SELECT $__timeGroup(created_at, $__interval) AS time, SUM(cost_usd)::float AS "USD" FROM cost_logs WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Page-views received (drift detector)
panels.description: Page-view beacon hits over the global dashboard time range. Powered by the audit_log drift detector (event_type='page_view_received') written by routes/cms_routes.py::track_page_view on every successful beacon. If this flatlines to 0 while Cloudflare reports traffic, the beacon path has regressed — same silent-failure mode that ran from 2026-04-09 to 2026-05-27. Red at 0, yellow at <10, green at 10+.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Page-views received" FROM audit_log WHERE event_type = 'page_view_received' AND $__timeFilter(timestamp)

panels.title: media_approvals — pending
panels.description: Pending media_approvals rows in the dashboard time window (filtered on created_at — pending rows have no decided_at). PR #647 lit up this table; 177 rows live as of 2026-05-28.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Pending" FROM media_approvals WHERE status='pending' AND $__timeFilter(created_at);

panels.title: media_approvals — approved (window)
panels.description: Approved media_approvals in the dashboard time window, filtered on decided_at.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Approved" FROM media_approvals WHERE status='approved' AND $__timeFilter(decided_at);

panels.title: media_approvals — rejected by medium
panels.description: Rejected media_approvals grouped by medium (podcast / video / video_short) in the dashboard time window. Filtered on decided_at.

targets.rawSql: SELECT medium AS metric, COUNT(*)::int AS value FROM media_approvals WHERE status='rejected' AND $__timeFilter(decided_at) GROUP BY medium ORDER BY value DESC;

panels.title: Posts with shot list (window)
panels.description: Posts where the video director produced a non-null video_shot_list, filtered on posts.updated_at within the dashboard time window. PR #650.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "With shot list" FROM posts WHERE video_shot_list IS NOT NULL AND $__timeFilter(updated_at);

panels.title: Director: shot list produced
panels.description: audit_log rows where event_type='video_director.shot_list_produced' in the dashboard time window. Event emit ships in a sibling PR — until then this reads zero.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Produced" FROM audit_log WHERE event_type='video_director.shot_list_produced' AND $__timeFilter(timestamp);

panels.title: Director: shot list failed
panels.description: audit_log rows where event_type='video_director.shot_list_failed' in the dashboard time window. Any nonzero count is a regression signal.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Failed" FROM audit_log WHERE event_type='video_director.shot_list_failed' AND $__timeFilter(timestamp);

panels.title: Media auto-rejected by quality (window)
panels.description: media_approvals rows the Layer 1 quality gate auto-rejected (decided_by LIKE 'auto:layer1%'). PR #648 wired this gate to write quality_score on every decision.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Auto-rejected" FROM media_approvals WHERE decided_by LIKE 'auto:layer1%' AND status='rejected' AND $__timeFilter(decided_at);

panels.title: Quality eval score by medium
panels.description: Per-medium quality_score from media_approvals over time. quality_evaluated_at drives the x-axis; one series per medium (podcast / video / video_short). PR #648.

targets.rawSql: SELECT quality_evaluated_at AS time, medium AS metric, quality_score::float AS value FROM media_approvals WHERE quality_score IS NOT NULL AND $__timeFilter(quality_evaluated_at) ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Tasks approved-but-already-published (drift)
panels.description: Snapshot drift counter — pipeline_tasks rows whose linked post is published but whose own status hasn't been promoted to published. Should always be 0; nonzero means PR #653's lockstep sync regressed. Intentionally ignores the dashboard time picker.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Drift" FROM pipeline_tasks pt JOIN posts p ON p.metadata->>'pipeline_task_id' = pt.task_id::text WHERE p.status='published' AND pt.status != 'published';

panels.title: LLM spend by provider (window)
panels.description: USD spend per provider in the dashboard time window. Local providers (ollama / electricity / local / system / compositor.ffmpeg_local) excluded so any wedge here represents real cloud spend.

targets.rawSql: SELECT provider AS metric, COALESCE(SUM(cost_usd),0)::float AS value FROM cost_logs WHERE provider NOT IN ('ollama','local','system','electricity','compositor.ffmpeg_local') AND $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY provider ORDER BY value DESC;

panels.title: Cloud fallback fires (window)
panels.description: cost_logs rows from non-local providers in the dashboard time window — every row is a cloud-fallback fire. Per feedback_cost_controls, treat any sustained nonzero as a signal to inspect.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Cloud fires" FROM cost_logs WHERE provider NOT IN ('ollama','local','system','electricity','compositor.ffmpeg_local') AND $__timeFilter(created_at);

panels.title: Spend vs daily limit
panels.description: Today's cost_logs.cost_usd sum as a percentage of app_settings.daily_spend_limit_usd. Orange at 80%. Intentionally uses CURRENT_DATE (a calendar-day snapshot) rather than the dashboard time picker — the limit is a daily budget.

targets.rawSql: SELECT CASE WHEN COALESCE(lim.value::numeric, 0) = 0 THEN 0 ELSE LEAST(100, ROUND((used.spend / lim.value::numeric) * 100, 1))::float END AS "% of daily limit" FROM (SELECT COALESCE(SUM(cost_usd),0)::numeric AS spend FROM cost_logs WHERE created_at::date = CURRENT_DATE) used CROSS JOIN (SELECT value FROM app_settings WHERE key='daily_spend_limit_usd') lim;

panels.title: Image style mix (window)
panels.description: Featured image style mix grouped by audit_log details->>'style'. NOTE: event_type='image_style_picked' is not yet emitted (verified 2026-05-28 — zero rows match). A sibling PR will add the emit hook in the featured-image stage; until then this reads zero.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COALESCE(details->>'style', 'unknown') AS metric, COUNT(*)::int AS value FROM audit_log WHERE event_type='image_style_picked' AND $__timeFilter(timestamp) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY value DESC;

panels.title: GlitchTip — error tracking
panels.description: GlitchTip Postgres is not provisioned as a Grafana datasource (separate container, no datasource yml). Open the GlitchTip UI for live unresolved-issue counts; a future PR can add it via Grafana's Infinity plugin once installed.

options.content:
### GlitchTip

**Unresolved issues:** open the UI for live counts.

- [Open GlitchTip dashboard](http://localhost:8080/glad-labs/poindexter/issues/)
- Org: `glad-labs` · Project: `poindexter`

_Live stat panel pending: Grafana Infinity plugin (or a glitchtip-postgres datasource provision) — neither is installed yet._

panels.title: Langfuse — traces + judge cost
panels.description: Langfuse Postgres (and Clickhouse, if v3 is wired) is not provisioned as a Grafana datasource. Open the Langfuse UI to drill into traces and judge-model spend; a future PR can add live stats via Infinity or a langfuse-postgres datasource.

options.content:
### Langfuse

**Traces + judge cost:** open the UI for live data.

- [Open Langfuse dashboard](http://localhost:3010/)
- Traces, prompts, datasets, experiment scores

_Live stat panels pending: Grafana Infinity plugin (or a langfuse-postgres datasource provision) — neither is installed yet._

title: Mission Control
